title: Who I Need in My Wealth Team
subtitle: A journal prompt set for getting specific about the advisors, mentors, and partners your building actually needs
excerpt: A four-prompt journal set from Day 74 of a 12-week money programme. The prompts move from identifying who you need in your world, to getting clear on the qualities you are looking for, to naming the support that is already in your circle.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 74 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when wealth building is moving faster than the support structure around it, or when you sense you need people in your corner but haven't named who or why.

content.text: A four-prompt journal set from Day 74 of a 12-week money programme. Day 74 theme: attracting the right advisors and deals. The prompts move from identifying who you need in your world, toward naming the qualities you will look for in those relationships, and finding what is already available to you. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
Who do you need in your world to help you build and sustain your legacy? Name the roles: lawyers, accountants, mentors, collaborators.
Write the list. Then write who is already there and who is still missing.
What energy or qualities will you look for in those relationships?
Not credentials only. Write what the relationship would feel like from the inside. What would tell you the person is aligned with your vision?
How can you trust yourself to recognise and choose only aligned support?
Write what you already know about your own judgement in choosing people. Where has it served you? What has it taught you?
Who in your current circle has already guided, supported, or inspired you toward smart wealth decisions?
Name the person. Write what they gave you. Name the next person you are ready to call in.
Adapted from the Day 74 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
